creator: h.schmalzbauer@OmniLAN.de
text:
Building bat (bacula-bat) fails with
... bacula-5.0.1/src/qt-console
make[1]: *** No rule to make target `all'.  Stop.

fix:
--- /dev/null	2010-03-17 12:56:22.000000000 +0100
+++ /usr/ports/sysutils/bacula-server/files/patch-configure	2010-03-17 
12:53:58.000000000 +0100
@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+--- configure.orig	2010-03-17 12:51:59.000000000 +0100
++++ configure	2010-03-17 12:52:14.000000000 +0100
+@@ -31880,7 +31880,7 @@
+       as_fn_error "Could not find qmake $PATH. Check your Qt 
installation" "$LINENO" 5
+    fi
+
+-   QMAKEBIN="qmake"
++   QMAKEBIN="$QMAKE"
+    cd src/qt-console
+    echo "Creating bat Makefile"
+    touch bat

There's another problem in Makefile:
LIB_DEPENDS+=   qwt.5:${PORTSDIR}/x11-toolkits/qwt5 \
                 bac.1:${PORTSDIR}/sysutils/bacula-client

libbac.so.1 doesn't exist anymore, there's libbac.so in /usr/local/lib.
But changing bac.1: to bac: doesn't work for me. What am I missing? 
Commenting out that LIB_DEPENDS starts and finishes build successfully. 
Please tke someone a look who has more knwoledge about LIB_DEPENDS
